response: {"purpose":"Figures 3a–b characterise the scalability and technical performance of the eCLIP protocol across a large panel of RNA-binding protein (RBP) target identification experiments conducted in two human cell lines (K562 and HepG2). Panel (a) uses pie charts to summarise experimental success rates for 209 eCLIP experiments, categorised by the amount of amplification (eCT) needed to generate sufficient sequencing library, showing that 102 experiments met validation metrics and were deposited in the ENCODE repository. Panel (b) plots each successful experiment as a point comparing the eCT of the best replicate against the drop in efficiency in the second replicate, with IgG-only negative controls included as reference, thereby revealing how library complexity and replicate consistency vary across the dataset. Together, these panels matter because they demonstrate that eCLIP can be reliably deployed at scale across hundreds of RBPs while maintaining quality standards, establishing the method's suitability for systematic, consortium-level functional genomics efforts.","title":"eCLIP experimental success rates and replicate efficiency across 209 experiments"}
